file_insights.auth_repository.dart: Added performBasicTokenRefresh() method to interface for internal use by TokenRefreshService
file_insights.auth_repository_impl.dart: Major refactor - added RefreshQueueManager dependency, implemented queue-based refresh fallback to basic refresh, added _saveSessionToStorage() helper method
file_insights.token_refresh_service.dart: Updated to call performBasicTokenRefresh() instead of refreshToken() to avoid circular dependency
file_insights.build-progress.txt: Updated to mark Phase 1.4 as completed

patterns_discovered: Dependency injection pattern with optional dependencies (RefreshQueueManager?)
Fallback pattern when robust refresh isn't available
Circular dependency avoidance by separating concerns
Completer-based request queuing for refresh operations
Debug logging throughout the refresh flow
gotchas_discovered: Circular dependency risk between TokenRefreshService and AuthRepository
Backward compatibility requirement when adding new dependencies
Need to preserve all session data (accessToken, refreshToken, expiresAt, idToken) during storage
Error propagation must be handled consistently across refresh strategies
discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS - Successfully integrated TokenRefreshService into AuthRepository while maintaining backward compatibility. Implementation uses optional dependency injection pattern with graceful fallback to basic refresh when queue manager is unavailable.
recommendations: Add comprehensive unit tests for both refresh strategies
Consider adding integration tests for the complete refresh flow
Add metrics to monitor refresh success rates and fallback usage
Consider adding logging for refresh strategy selection
